fights.fighter_a_bio: Topuria hunts the chin with volume striking while staying defensive—he throws 6+ strikes per minute but doesn't overcommit. His wrestling is active (lands takedowns to control rounds), and he's proven he can survive and counter heavy strikers, making him a clear Vegas and model favorite at -518.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Gaethje is a heavy front-foot striker who relies on leg kicks, body shots, and high-volume combos to wear opponents down. He struggles against pressure and gets caught countering when he lunges—our model gives him just 19.1% here despite his knockout power.
fights.watch_for: If Gaethje plants and trades with Topuria early, he risks getting countered into combinations. If Topuria clinches and lands knees, Gaethje will gas trying to escape—that's how Ilia wins decisively.

fights.fighter_a_bio: Pereira is a precision striker who hunts the counter—he waits for openings, then explodes with devastating knees and crosses. He's dangerous at range but vulnerable to pressure fighters who cut the cage and force him into exchanges where his timing breaks down.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Gane is a 6'5" heavyweight who uses his length and footwork to control space, peppering opponents with kicks and jabs while staying just outside their reach. He's a massive favorite here: his model prediction is 94.7% because he can dictate distance all night, and Pereira's counter-striking game falls apart when he can't set his feet.
fights.watch_for: Can Pereira close the distance without eating a Gane leg kick or straight? If Gane maintains the perimeter and makes Pereira chase, it's a long night for the underdog.

fights.fighter_a_bio: O'Malley picks you apart with precision striking—sharp hands, creative footwork, and the range to land clean while staying out of danger. He's a massive favorite here because his striking defense and distance control consistently frustrate opponents who can't close the gap cleanly.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Zahabi relies on clinch work and top control when he can get inside, but he's eating significant strikes on the way in. His wrestling can turn the fight, but O'Malley's footwork makes it hard for him to create those opportunities.
fights.watch_for: Can Zahabi close the distance and lock up O'Malley in the clinch, or will O'Malley's footwork and range keep Zahabi at striking distance where he's outgunned?

fights.fighter_a_bio: Josh Hokit is Vegas's pick here, but the betting odds don't match what the model sees. He'll need to dictate this fight early and avoid getting drawn into Derrick Lewis's power range.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Derrick Lewis is the model's pick at 64.2% — a significant edge. He hunts for the knockout with explosive striking, and his Elo rating reflects a fighter who's proven against better competition.
fights.watch_for: Whether Hokit can stay composed and keep distance early, or if Lewis lands clean and forces him to panic into the pocket where Lewis's raw power becomes the deciding factor.

fights.fighter_a_bio: Ruffy's a submission threat who hunts the takedown early and finishes on the ground—he's won 5 of his last 6 by tap-out or ref stoppage. Vegas and our model heavily favor him here, seeing a fighter riding momentum against someone he should control.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Chandler throws heat from the jump with heavy hands and explosive takedowns, but he's dropped recent decisions and struggles when opponents keep him at distance. He needs early chaos and forward pressure to avoid getting outwrestled.
fights.watch_for: Can Chandler land something devastating in the first 90 seconds before Ruffy clinches and drags him down? If the fight hits round 2, Ruffy's grappling control usually takes over.

fights.fighter_a_bio: Bo Nickal is a wrestling machine who suffocates opponents with top control and ground-and-pound, but his striking is a massive liability and he hasn't faced elite competition yet. Vegas loves him here, but he's surprisingly vulnerable to anyone who can keep the fight standing or stuff his takedowns early.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Kyle Daukaus hunts for submissions constantly—he's a guillotine and rear-naked choke specialist who stays dangerous even when losing on his back. Our model heavily favors him (78.1%) because his submission threats create openings everywhere, and he's proven at this level.
fights.watch_for: If Nickal secures a takedown in round one and holds top position for 5+ minutes, he can control the fight. If Daukaus stuffs even two takedown attempts and drags Nickal into the second round where wrestling fatigue sets in, his submission game takes over.

fights.fighter_a_bio: Diego Lopes hunts with volume striking and feints to set up takedowns—he's comfortable pressuring opponents both standing and on the mat. Vegas and our model heavily favor him (93.8% win probability), largely because his Elo rating lags behind Garcia's despite being the sharper, more proven finisher.
fights.fighter_b_bio: Steve Garcia is a pure striker who relies on timing and precision rather than output—he needs clean looks to be effective. His higher Elo suggests past success, but he's a massive underdog here (6.2% win probability) because Lopes' ability to mix levels exploits strikers who can't defend the clinch and takedown threat.
fights.watch_for: Whether Garcia can stay vertical and land counters before Lopes closes distance and forces the fight into his comfort zone on the mat or against the cage.
